intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Dark backgrounds with stripped-down layouts reduce visual noise and make cooking feel less overwhelming
facade.label: Minimalist dark
facade.acceptImplies: User may prefer calm, low-friction interfaces that feel anti-chef-ish; likes simplicity over decoration
facade.rejectImplies: User may want warmth, color, or more visual guidance; finds stark minimalism cold or uninviting

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to soft, inviting interfaces that feel encouraging rather than clinical or demanding
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use warm color palettes, soft lighting effects, gentle typography, and an encouraging tone that feels like a friend helping you cook, not a stern instructor
facade.rejectImplies: The app should lean toward cooler tones, sharper contrast, minimal embellishment, and a more direct/efficient interface that removes friction without coziness

evidence.hypothesis: Dark backgrounds with stripped-down layouts reduce visual noise and make cooking feel less overwhelming
evidence.implication: User may prefer calm, low-friction interfaces that feel anti-chef-ish; likes simplicity over decoration

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer seeing one recipe at a time, large and focused, rather than browsing lists or grids
facade.label: One big recipe card
facade.acceptImplies: The app should favor full-screen or near-full-screen recipe views, minimal competing elements, reduce cognitive load by hiding alternatives during active viewing
facade.rejectImplies: Users want to see multiple recipes simultaneously—grid view, sidebar browsing, or comparison mode might be more important than focus. Density and scanability matter more than single-item clarity

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a directive, almost bossy tone that removes decision fatigue rather than collaborative or suggestive framing
facade.axisTargeted: friendly vs professional + playful vs serious
facade.label: Just tell me what to cook
facade.content: Just tell me what to cook
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should be commanding and clear, removing cognitive load. Less 'What sounds good?' more 'Make this tonight.' Branded personality that owns the kitchen.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want softer guidance, collaborative tone, or more options presented. Voice should be suggestive rather than directive. Neutral or warm rather than bold.

facade.hypothesis: Users will prefer gradual ingredient reveals (animated list) over instant full-view for recipes they find intimidating
facade.label: Ingredients fade in
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe complexity should be revealed gradually; animations reduce cognitive overwhelm for cooking-averse users
facade.rejectImplies: Users want immediate full recipe visibility; animations might feel gimmicky or slow; static instant lists are more trustworthy

facade.hypothesis: Users hate recipes because they're overwhelmed by ingredient lists and method steps. Showing ONLY prep/cook time upfront (not full recipe density) reduces cognitive load and helps quick decision-making.
facade.label: Just the time
facade.acceptImplies: User wants minimal information architecture — show only what's needed to decide, hide detail behind interactions, favor quick-scan metrics over comprehensive data display.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants confidence before committing — needs ingredient count, servings, difficulty, or nutrition visible upfront to justify the time investment, even if it means more visual density.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
Whether user needs extensive prep info and substitution options vs. minimal decision-making and pre-curated choices
axes.poleA: All options or just pick one

axes.label: Whether user prefers instant feedback and progress celebration vs. quiet, frictionless interaction with no fanfare

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs extensive prep info and substitution options vs. minimal decision-making and pre-curated choices
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant feedback and progress celebration vs. quiet, frictionless interaction with no fanfare
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to vibrant, energetic visuals that gamify the experience and make it feel fun rather than like a chore
facade.label: Bright and colorful
facade.acceptImplies: User may want encouragement through visual delight; recipes feel like play, not work; tolerates bold UI
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer restraint; fears overwhelming visual stimulation; wants calm over excitement; leans toward minimalist aesthetic

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in under 15 minutes with minimal ingredients and zero fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so simple, you'll actually cook them

Filter by mood

Today's picks

Garlic butter pasta

8 min4 ingredients

Boil pasta. Melt butter with garlic. Toss. Done. The kind of meal that tastes like you tried.

Avocado toast

3 min3 ingredients

Toast bread. Smash avocado. Sprinkle salt. Eat standing up at the counter like a normal person.

Egg fried rice

10 min5 ingredients

Leftover rice, scrambled eggs, soy sauce, frozen veg. Stir in a pan. Tastes like takeout.

Out of ideas?

Get a random recipe that takes less time than scrolling TikTok
